title: Creating Base Templates: Establishing a consistent layout across pages using base templates

content.problemStatement:
In Flask web development, creating a consistent layout across multiple pages is crucial for maintaining a cohesive user experience. \
This is where base templates come into play. \
A base template serves as a foundation for other templates, allowing you to define a common structure that can be shared across different pages of your website.

Building on your knowledge of Jinja2 templating, which we covered earlier, base templates take advantage of template inheritance to create a modular and efficient approach to structuring your views.

Here's how base templates work:

1. Create a base template: \
Start by creating a file, typically named "base.html", which will contain the common elements of your website such as the HTML structure, head section, navigation bar, and footer.

2. Use placeholder blocks: \
Within your base template, you'll define placeholder blocks using Jinja2 syntax. \
These blocks represent areas that can be customized in child templates. \
For example:

   ```html
   
   
   
       {% block title %}Default Title{% endblock %}
   
   
       
           
       
       
       {% block content %}
       
       {% endblock %}
       
       
           
       
   
   
   ```

3. Extend the base template: \
In your child templates, you'll extend the base template and override the blocks as needed.\
For example, in a "home.html" template:

   ```html
   {% extends "base.html" %}

   {% block title %}Home Page{% endblock %}

   {% block content %}
   Welcome to our website!
   This is the home page content.
   {% endblock %}
   ```

4. Render the template:
In your Flask routes, you'll render these child templates as usual. \
Flask and Jinja2 will automatically combine the base template with the child template:

   ```python
   @app.route('/')
   def home():
       return render_template('home.html')
   ```

By using base templates, you ensure that common elements remain consistent across your website while allowing for easy customization of specific sections on individual pages. \
Remember, you can have multiple levels of template inheritance, allowing for even more flexibility in structuring your views.
